key_insight: A distribution-agnostic reinforcement-learning wrapper robustifies a nominal trajectory by learning affine feedback corrections that satisfy chance constraints using only sampleable uncertainty.
engineering_relevance: The approach offers a reusable control scaffold for robust spacecraft and rocket trajectory planning when uncertainty models are incomplete or non-Gaussian.
method: Compute a deterministic nominal trajectory offline.
Learn feedforward corrections plus time-varying feedback gains with reinforcement learning.
Enforce probabilistic feasibility with rollout-based upper-tail quantiles and terminal covariance penalties.
evidence_signal: The framework is evaluated on two diverse problems—a 3D Earth-Mars transfer and a stochastic atmospheric rocket landing—and tested under Gaussian, bounded uniform, and unseen disturbance settings.
watch_item: Performance depends on sampled rollouts and constraint tuning, so feasibility and cost may be sensitive to training coverage and distribution shift.
paper_title: Distribution-Agnostic Robust Trajectory Optimization via Chance-Constrained Reinforcement Learning
